meta.title: MergeCore TypeScript Rules
meta.description: Rules for TypeScript: strictness, narrowing, sound generics, safe assertions, and API boundary typing.
meta.notes: Gate with typescript: true. Many rules assume strict mode; confirm tsconfig compatibility before flagging.

error.label: High risk (type escape or runtime crash)

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one TypeScript file), sum penalties after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Sound types, narrow boundaries, no any leakage.
interpretation.7_to_8: Solid; tighten a few assertions or widen input validation.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: any sprinkled in, non-null assertions without proof, unchecked JSON.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: unsafe API boundaries or pervasive type escapes.

rules.title: Use of any escapes the type system
rules.description: Prefer unknown with explicit narrowing for untrusted data. Reserve any for well-documented, temporary boundary code.

examples.after: function parse(input: unknown): string { if (typeof input === 'object' && input && 'value' in input) return String((input as { value: unknown }).value); throw new Error('bad input'); }

rules.title: Non-null assertion without runtime proof
rules.description: The ! operator silences the type checker but not runtime. Prefer guards, invariant(), or proper narrowing.

heuristics: Asserted value comes from a lookup, find, or optional chain

examples.before: const user = users.find(u => u.id === id)!; return user.email;
examples.after: const user = users.find(u => u.id === id); if (!user) throw new Error('not found'); return user.email;

rules.title: Structural cast from untrusted input
rules.description: Casting JSON or form data with as T trusts the producer without verification. Validate with a schema (zod, valibot, io-ts) at the boundary.

rules.title: Disabled strict null checks in surface code
rules.description: Turning off strictNullChecks hides real defects. If a single file needs escape hatches, scope them narrowly rather than weakening config.

rules.title: Generic parameter unused or unconstrained
rules.description: A generic with no usage or no bound offers no safety. Drop it, constrain it (extends), or accept the concrete type.

heuristics: Type parameter not used in argument or return

rules.title: Numeric enum where string union suffices
rules.description: Numeric enums generate runtime objects and permit unintended arithmetic. Prefer const union types for stable, serialisable values.

rules.title: External response typed without validation
rules.description: Assigning fetch().json() to a typed variable trusts upstream shape. Validate at the boundary to contain breakage.

rules.title: Floating promise (no await, no handler)
rules.description: Unawaited promises swallow errors and reorder execution. Await, return, or attach a terminal handler.

heuristics: Async function called without await, return, or .catch at statement position

rules.title: Overuse of object or Object type
rules.description: The Object/object types are rarely what you mean and allow unsafe property access. Prefer a named interface or Record<string, T>.

rules.title: Strong pattern: user-defined type guard
rules.description: Predicate functions with `x is T` return types make narrowing explicit and auditable.

rules.title: Deeply conditional types for simple mapping
rules.description: Complex conditional types increase compile time and cognitive load. Use a straightforward interface when the mapping is fixed.

heuristics: Three or more nested conditional layers

rules.title: Type-only import not marked as type
rules.description: import type avoids emitting imports that have runtime side effects and clarifies intent. Helps bundlers and isolatedModules.

heuristics: Imported binding is used only in type positions
